mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and eases financial conditions, so sustained month-over-month declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS has declined for two consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates enough to force a tighter FEDFUNDS path, real discount rates rise and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is at or above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not deliver negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the average triggered return is not at least 0.5 standard deviations below unconditional SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and deteriorating growth expectations, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and pressures S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if monthly unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50% for any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in PAYEMS and increases in UNRATE identify a broad labor-market slowdown that transmits to weaker revenue expectations and lower equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls over 3 months while UNRATE rises over the same period
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-slowdown condition does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the average return is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS signals stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of monthly observations, or if the Rank IC is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST reflects tightening housing demand and weaker cyclical activity, which leads equity investors to price slower growth and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are followed by nonnegative 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is stable or falling, real activity is expanding faster than inflation pressure, which supports margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises over 2 months and CPIAUCSL is flat or lower over the same period
failure_condition: Reject if the combined industrial-production/disinflation condition fails to outperform unconditional SPX returns, or if positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, strong INDPRO and low unemployment indicate resilient real activity that reduces recession risk; under this regime, the signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice downside risk downward.
failure_condition: Reject if, during periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.0%, high INDPRO and low UNRATE do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal reverses sign outside tightening regimes.
